generatedHeader: AUTO-GENERATED — Do not edit by hand. Source: platform/types/catalog.json. Run: node scripts/codegen/types.mjs

SystemEventType.doc:
Every event the platform may emit. The rule engine matches `Rule.trigger
.eventType` against this enum.

Cases marked **(V1)** have a TriggerEvaluator implementation in
`_shared/ruleEngine.ts`. Other cases are declared so the model stays
V4-ready; the engine ignores rules whose trigger is not implemented yet.

values.name: eventClosed
values.doc:
(V1) Host pressed "Cerrar evento" — fires `evaluate-event-rules`
immediately so proposed fines appear without cron lag.

values.name: eventCreated
values.doc:
Event was created. Not used by V1 rules but emitted on every create
so analytics + future rules can hook in.

values.name: rsvpDeadlinePassed
values.doc: (V1) RSVP deadline passed — synthetic, scheduled by cron.

values.name: hoursBeforeEvent
values.doc:
(V1) Synthetic — emitted by the cron N hours before an event starts,
so rules like "host sin menú" can check at -24h.

values.name: rsvpSubmitted
values.doc: (V1) Member submitted or updated their RSVP.

values.name: rsvpChangedSameDay
values.doc: (V1) Member changed their RSVP after it became "same-day".

values.name: checkInRecorded
values.doc: (V1) Someone arrived (host or self check-in).

values.name: checkInMissed
values.doc:
Confirmed-going member never checked in — synthetic, computed at
event close time. V1 handles via `eventClosed` + `checkInExists=false`
condition; this case exists for future rules that want a dedicated
trigger.

values.name: eventDescriptionMissing
values.doc:
Host hasn't filled in the event description/menú yet. Used by the
optional V1 rule "Anfitrión sin menú" (default OFF).

values.name: fineReminderSent
values.doc: Cron emitted reminder for an unpaid fine at 3/7/14 day thresholds.

values.name: voteOpened
values.doc: (V1) Generic vote opened — emitted by `start_vote` RPC.

values.name: voteCast
values.doc: (V1) Member cast their ballot — emitted by `cast_vote` RPC.

values.name: voteResolved
values.doc:
(V1) Generic vote finalized — emitted by `finalize_vote` RPC. Carries
`payload.resolution`: `passed` | `failed` | `quorum_failed`.

ConditionType.doc:
Every condition the rule engine knows how to evaluate. Rules combine
multiple conditions with AND.

Cases marked **(V1)** have a ConditionEvaluator implementation in
`_shared/ruleEngine.ts`. Other cases throw `NotImplementedError` server-
side; rules using them are skipped with a structured log line.

values.name: alwaysTrue
values.doc: (V1) Always true. Used when a rule has no preconditions.

values.name: responseStatusIs
values.doc:
(V1) Member RSVP equals a configured status.
Config: `{ "status": "pending" | "going" | "maybe" | "declined" | "waitlisted" }`

values.name: checkInExists
values.doc:
(V1) A check-in row exists / does not exist for the member.
Config: `{ "exists": true | false }`

values.name: checkInMinutesLate
values.doc:
(V1) Minutes late at check-in vs the event start time.
Config: `{ "thresholdMinutes": Int }` — true when `lateMinutes >= threshold`.

values.name: eventDescriptionMissing
values.doc: (V1) Event description / menú is empty.

values.name: minutesAfterScheduled
values.doc: X minutes after the event's scheduled start time.

values.name: hoursBeforeEvent
values.doc:
X hours before the event starts. Used as a synthetic trigger config
rather than a condition in V1.

ConsequenceType.doc:
Every consequence the rule engine can execute when a rule's conditions
match. Rules can chain multiple consequences (all execute).

Cases marked **(V1)** have a ConsequenceExecutor implementation in
`_shared/ruleEngine.ts`. Other cases throw `NotImplementedError`; rules
using them are skipped with a structured log line so the architecture
stays V4-ready without silently failing in production.

values.name: fine
values.doc:
(V1) Create a row in `fines` table with status `proposed`. The
fine_review_periods row gives host 24h to review before auto-
officializing.
Config (flat fee): `{ "amount": 200 }`
Config (escalating): `{ "baseAmount": 200, "stepAmount": 50, "stepMinutes": 30 }`

values.name: event
values.doc:
V1 — the only implemented type. Lives in `events` table; queried via
`events_view` which projects to a Resource shape.

values.name: slot
values.precedingComment:
V2+ types — declared here so the platform model is V4-ready. Edge
function rule engine ignores types it doesn't know about; Swift code
throws a clear error if it sees one before the matching template
ships.

GovernanceAction.doc:
Governance action evaluated by `GovernanceService`. Each case maps to one
key in `groups.governance` jsonb. Stable raw values — these are part of
the API surface for SQL helper functions like `group_governance_level`.

PermissionLevel.doc:
Permission level applied to a governance action. Stored as raw string in
`groups.governance` jsonb. The values mirror the enum cases verbatim
(camelCase on disk to match the migration backfill).

values.name: founder
values.doc: Only the founder of the group can perform the action.

values.name: anyMember
values.doc: Any active member of the group can perform the action.

values.name: majorityVote
values.doc: A successful majority vote (>= 50% threshold by default) is required.

values.name: supermajorityVote
values.doc: A successful supermajority vote (>= 66% threshold by default) is required.

values.name: host
values.doc:
Only the host of the contextual event can perform the action.
Useful for actions like `closeEvents` where the actor is determined
by the resource being acted on.

values.name: treasurer
values.doc: Only the treasurer (V2 role) can perform the action.
